results.name: travel:international_no_road
results.message: 425 chars, no tool expected, matches expected pattern
results.responseText:
you can’t drive it straight up, there’s no road route from sydney to auckland (you’d have to cross the tasman sea).

---
if you mean “door to door”:
**fly time:** ~3 hours sydney → auckland (plus airport time) 
**typical total:** ~6–8 hours all up depending on check-in/bags/traffic

if you tell me where in sydney you’re starting from (suburb) and where in auckland you’re headed, i can give a tighter door-to-door estimate.

results.name: places:coffee_specific
results.responseText:
here are a few solid picks in fitzroy.

---
**industry beans fitzroy** — 4.4/5 (1,991)
70-76 westgarth st. (currently closed)
big warehouse vibe, proper roastery setup, consistently good espresso + great brunch.
https://industrybeans.com/pages/fitzroy-menu

---
**faraday’s cage** — 4.5/5 (937)
325-329 gore st. (currently closed)
reliable “coffee-first” place, good for a quieter sit and a solid long black.
http://www.faradayscage.com.au/

---
**stagger lee’s fitzroy** — 4.5/5 (850)
276 brunswick st. (currently closed)
single origin focus, good toasties, classic brunswick st people-watching.
https://staggerlees.com.au/

---
**slowpoke espresso** — 4.6/5 (445)
157 brunswick st. (currently closed)
cosy courtyard, great if you want a slower breakfast and an easy hang.
http://www.facebook.com/pages/Slowpoke-Espresso/221714874514535

---
**blackcat fitzroy** — 4.6/5 (1,190)
252 brunswick st. **open now**
more of a bar vibe later, but genuinely good coffee and a nice window seat.
http://www.blackcatfitzroy.com/

if you tell me what you’re after (quick takeaway vs sit-down, espresso vs filter), i’ll narrow it to the best 1 or 2.

results.name: places:restaurant_area
results.responseText:
here are a few solid picks around flinders lane.

---
**cha ching** — 4.7/5 (2.3k)
348 flinders ln. open now. 
fun, loud, asian-fusion with big flavours. good for groups. 

---
**nomad melbourne** — 4.5/5 (1.2k)
187 flinders ln. open now. 
wood-fired, modern aussie/med vibes, great wine list. a good “nice dinner” option.

---
**supernormal** — 4.5/5 (5.1k) $$$
180 flinders ln. open now. 
buzzy bench seating, asian-inspired menu. iconic for a reason (can be a wait).

---
**farmer’s daughters** — 4.5/5 (1.9k) $$$
95 exhibition st (2 mins off flinders ln). open now. 
seasonal local produce, plus a rooftop bar if you want a drink after.

---
**tippy-tay** — 4.3/5 (1.2k) $$
101 flinders ln. open now. 
italian-ish, good for a casual date night and a spritz.

if you tell me the vibe (quick bite vs date night vs group) and budget, i’ll narrow it to the best 1-2.

results.name: places:specific_place
results.responseText:
People generally rave about it. The consensus is usually that the croissants are some of the best you'll find anywhere, though you'll almost always be dealing with a decent queue if you go during peak times.

Most people say the "classic" Lune croissant is worth the wait, and the cruffins are a big draw if they haven't sold out. It’s definitely a "treat yourself" kind of spot rather than a quick grab-and-go.

Are you thinking of heading to the one in the city or Fitzroy?

next open depends on the day (some days are night market etc) but if you tell me when you’re thinking of going, i’ll pin the exact hours.

results.name: format:how do i get from richmond to the city b
results.responseText:
easiest tram run is off swan st.

---
**best tram option (swan st)**
**walk to:** Church St/Swan St stop
**line:** Tram 70 (towards Waterfront City Docklands) 
**ride to:** Flinders Street Station (Stop 4) 
**time:** ~18 mins on the tram (about ~25-35 mins door-to-door depending where you start in richmond)

---
**backup (also swan st)**
**line:** Tram 75 (towards Melbourne Uni) 
**ride to:** Flinders Street / Swanston St area, then it’s a short walk into the cbd

---
if you tell me roughly where you are in richmond (swan st end vs victoria st end), i’ll point you at the closest stop + the best line from there.
